tool_name: SMED Setup Time Reduction, Capacity Recovery & EOQ Optimisation (Shingo / Harris)

inputs.name: Current Internal Setup Time (machine stopped)
inputs.hint: From time study — time machine is non-productive during changeover

inputs.name: Current External Setup Time (pre/post while running)
inputs.hint: Preparation activities already done while machine is running previous job

inputs.name: Internal-to-External Conversion Achievement
inputs.hint: % of current internal time successfully converted to external via SMED programme

inputs.name: Setup Streamlining Reduction (remaining internal)
inputs.hint: Additional reduction from quick-clamps, standardisation, mistake-proofing of remaining internal time

inputs.name: Machine Opportunity Cost Rate (fully burdened)
inputs.hint: Cost of machine standing idle — includes depreciation, operator, overhead

inputs.name: Annual Inventory Holding Cost Rate
inputs.hint: Warehousing + capital tied up + obsolescence + insurance — typically 20–35% of unit value

formulas: Internal_After_Conversion_min = internal_setup_current_min * (1 - internal_to_external_conversion_pct / 100)   // [min] Internal time after conversion step
Internal_After_Streamlining_min = Internal_After_Conversion_min * (1 - streamlining_reduction_pct / 100)   // [min] Internal time after all SMED improvements
Target_Total_Setup_min = Internal_After_Streamlining_min   // [min] New internal setup time — the SMED target
Setup_Time_Saved_min = internal_setup_current_min - Target_Total_Setup_min   // [min] Internal time recovered per changeover
Annual_Capacity_Recovered_min = Setup_Time_Saved_min * changeovers_per_year   // [min/year] Machine time recovered
Current_EOQ_units = SQRT((2 * annual_demand_units * Current_Setup_Cost_USD) / Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD)   // [units] Economic Order Qty — current
New_EOQ_units = SQRT((2 * annual_demand_units * New_Setup_Cost_USD) / Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD)   // [units] Economic Order Qty — after SMED
Average_Inventory_Reduction_units = (Current_EOQ_units - New_EOQ_units) / 2   // [units] Reduction in average cycle stock
Inventory_Value_Released_USD = Average_Inventory_Reduction_units * unit_material_cost_usd   // [USD] Working capital released
Inventory_Cost_Saving_USD = Average_Inventory_Reduction_units * Holding_Cost_per_Unit_USD   // [USD/year] Annual holding cost saving
Total_Annual_Benefit_USD = Annual_Setup_Cost_Saving_USD + Inventory_Cost_Saving_USD   // [USD/year] Total financial benefit
Setup_Reduction_Pct = (Setup_Time_Saved_min / internal_setup_current_min) * 100   // [%] Overall setup reduction achieved

target_faster.error_msg: Both conversion and streamlining reductions are zero — no SMED improvement defined. Enter expected improvement percentages.

conversion_range.error_msg: Conversion rate cannot exceed 100% of internal time.

holding_rate_range.error_msg: Holding cost rate < 5% is unrealistically low. Minimum components: capital cost (WACC) + warehousing + insurance. Typical industrial: 20–30%.

smart_warnings.message: BATCH PRODUCTION MODE: Current EOQ exceeds 2 months of demand. High setup cost is forcing large batch sizes, creating excess WIP, long lead times, and poor flexibility. SMED is the correct intervention — reducing setup cost directly enables smaller, leaner batches.

Setup reduction < 50%: Below typical SMED first-pass target. Shingo's methodology targets >75% reduction in internal time. Review: Have all convertible internal activities been identified? Are quick-clamp fixtures and standardised toolholder systems being used?

Significant capacity recovered (>8 hours equivalent): At this machine rate, recovered capacity has high monetary value. If demand exists, this freed capacity can be converted to throughput rather than cost saving — model the revenue upside separately.
SMED enables weekly or sub-weekly batch sizes: Approaching one-piece-flow conditions. Consider implementing Pull / Kanban replenishment system to replace EOQ-based push scheduling.

Low conversion rate with long internal setup: SMED Stage 1 (separation of internal/external) is incomplete. Most setups > 30 min have 40–60% of activities convertible to external. Conduct video-based setup analysis to identify conversion opportunities.

WORKING CAPITAL RELEASE EXCEEDS PROJECT COST: Inventory value released from smaller batch sizes alone covers the SMED investment. Present to finance as a working capital project, not just a productivity initiative.
